subject: Chemistry
solution:
The complex that absorbs minimum wave length of light has maximum splitting

Δ0∝1λΔ0=Crystal field stabilization energy

Here, CN- being strong field ligand ,It will have cause CFSE So,Co(CN)63- will absorb minimum wavelength.

The hybridization of Co in Co(CN)63- will be d2sp3

As, the complex has no unpaired electrons, spin only  magnetic moment will be zero and it is diamagnetic in nature.

Ligand which has two different donor atoms and either of the two donor atom is attached to the metal during complex formation is called ambidentate ligand. Among the given ligands NCS-SCN- and  NO2-ONO- are  ambidentate ligands

C2O42- bidentate,ethylenediamine(bidentate) and H2O(monodentate) ligands are not ambidentate.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
In a tetrahedral complex, the five d-orbitals split into two sets due to the crystal field created by the ligands.

The ligands approach the central metal ion between the axes. The $d_{xy}$, $d_{yz}$, and $d_{xz}$ orbitals (forming the $t_2$ set) point between the axes, experiencing greater repulsion from the ligands, and thus have higher energy.

The $d_{x^2-y^2}$ and $d_{z^2}$ orbitals (forming the $e$ set) point along the axes, experiencing lesser repulsion, and thus have lower energy.

Therefore, the energy order is $d_{xy} = d_{yz} = d_{xz} > d_{x^2-y^2} = d_{z^2}$.

Evaluating the given statements:
A. $d_{xy} = d_{xz} > d_{x^2-y^2}$ is true.
B. $d_{xy} = d_{yz} > d_{z^2}$ is true.
C. $d_{x^2-y^2} > d_{z^2} > d_{xz}$ is false.
D. $d_{x^2-y^2} = d_{z^2} < d_{xz}$ is true.

Hence, statements A, B, and D are true.

Answer: A, B and D only

subject: Chemistry
solution:
A spin-only magnetic moment occurs when an electron rotates on its own axis and generates a magnetic field.

μ = n ( n + 2 )BM
Here, n is the number of unpaired electrons.
The unit for a spin-only magnetic moment is the Bohr magneton (BM).

MnH2O62+

Mn2+=3d5

μ=5(5+2)=5.91BM

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Ambidentate ligand has two or more donor atoms, but during complex formation, only one donor atom is attached to central metal. SCN− ion has donor atoms N and S. 

Examples of ambidentate ligands include NO2- and SO32-. So,4 SCN- ligands are ambidentate.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Silver nitrate on reaction of chloride ion gives silver chloride as precipitate. Chloride ions outside of coordination sphere will only give precipitates. Thus,
CoNH34Cl2Cl⇒ Gives 1 mole AgCl
NiH2O6Cl2⇒ Gives 2 moles AgCl
PtNH32Cl2⇒ Gives No AgCl
PdNH34Cl2⇒ Gives 2 moles AgCl
Total number of moles of AgCl=5 mole.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
(1) FeF63-: Fe3+=3d5 (ΔO<P)

Number of unpaired e-=5

μ = n(n+2) BM

∴μ=35 BM

(2) CoF63-: Co3+=3d6ΔO<P

Number of unpaired e-=4

μ = n(n+2) BM

∴μ=24 BM

(3) CoC2O433-: Co3+=3d6ΔO>P

Number of unpaired e-=0

μ = n(n+2) BM

∴μ=0 BM

So, the correct order is FeF63->CoF63->CoC2O433-

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Ligands that include a numerical prefix in the name use the prefixes bis for 2, tris for 3, or tetrakis for 4 to indicate their number.

If the complex ion is an anion, we drop the ending of the metal name and add –ate.

In the given coordination compound, Co is the cobalt metal, C2O42- are the ligands named as oxalato.

The charge on [Co(C2O4)3] is -3 and oxalate is having charge of -2.

X+3(-2)=-3X=+3

Hence, the IUPAC name of the compound K3[Co(C2O4)3] is Potassium trioxalatocobaltate(III).

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Statement A is incorrect as the complex is yellow-green, not red.
Statement B is incorrect because the complex shows low solubility in water, especially at neutral pH.
Statement C is incorrect because Ni(II) in square planar geometry has all d-electrons paired (d^8 configuration), not unpaired electrons.
Statement D is correct regarding the N-Ni-N bond angle being approximately 90° in square planar geometry.
Statement E is correct as the complex contains two five-membered metallacycles (chelate rings) per dimethylglyoxime molecule.
The incorrect statements are B, C, and E.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The t2g (triplet, lower energy) orbitals are the dxy, dxz and dyz orbitals. The eg (doublet, higher energy) orbitals are the dz2 and dx2-y2 orbitals. These split d-orbitals are called t2g and egbecause they are split by the effect of the surrounding ligand ions in a crystal field.

CrH2O63+ Contains Cr3+:Ar3d3:t2g3ego
FeH2O63+ Contains Fe3+:Ar3d5:t2g3eg2
NiH2O62+ Contains Ni2+:Ar3d8:t2g6eg2

VH2O63+ Contains V3+:Ar3d2:t2g2ego

subject: Chemistry
solution:
In $\left[\mathrm{Co}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right]_6\right]^{3+}, \mathrm{Co}^{+3}:[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^6, \mathrm{NH}_3$ is S.F.L
Hybridisation state of $\mathrm{Co}^{3+}$ is $\mathrm{d}^2 \mathrm{sp}^3$
In $\mathrm{SF}_6$, Hybridisation state of sulphur is $\mathrm{sp}^3 \mathrm{~d}^2$
In $\left[\mathrm{CrF}_6\right]^{3-}, \mathrm{Cr}^{+3}:[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^3$
Hybridisation state of $\mathrm{Cr}^{3+}$ is $\mathrm{d}^2 \mathrm{sp}^3$
$\left[\mathrm{CoF}_6\right]^{3-}, \mathrm{Co}^{+3}:[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^6 \mathrm{~F}^{-}$is W.F.L
Hybridisation state of $\mathrm{Co}^{3+}$ is $\mathrm{sp}^3 \mathrm{~d}^2$
$\left[\mathrm{Mn}(\mathrm{CN})_6\right]^{3-}, \mathrm{Mn}^{+3}:[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^4 \mathrm{CN}^{-}$is S.F.L Hybridisation state of $\mathrm{Mn}^{3+}$ is $\mathrm{d}^2 \mathrm{sp}^3$
$\left[\mathrm{MnCl}_6\right]^{3-}, \mathrm{Mn}^{+3}:[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^4 \mathrm{Cl}^{-}$is W.F.L
Hybridisation state of $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$is $\mathrm{sp}^3 \mathrm{~d}^2$
Total number of $\mathrm{sp}^3 \mathrm{~d}^2$ hybridized molecules is 3

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The chlorophyll molecule has a central magnesium atom engulfed by a nitrogen-containing structure called a porphyrin ring; attached to the ring is a long carbon-hydrogen side chain.

Hardness of water is determined by titrating with a standard solution of ethylene diamine tetra acetic acid (EDTA) which is a complexing agent. Since EDTA is insoluble in water, the disodium salt of EDTA is taken for this experiment. EDTA can form four or six coordination bonds with a metal ion.

Silver bromide is used in photography as a component of an emulsion that helps develop a photographic image. Silver bromide is sensitive to light, and when suspended in gelatin, silver bromide's grains create a photographic emulsion.

Wilkinson's catalyst is the common name for chlorido­tris(triphenylphosphine)­rhodium(I), a coordination complex of rhodium with the formula Ph3P3RhCl

D-Penicillamine is chelating ligand.

subject: Chemistry
solution: Non superimposable mirror images are called optical isomers and may be described as "chiral'. They are also called enantiomers and rotate plane polarised light in opposite directions.
